prevalence.source: ICMR / FOGSI guidance and obstetric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some states with rising maternal age, obesity, and diabetes

burden.mortalityRate: Significant contributor to maternal mortality where referral delays occur
burden.source: IHME GBD / WHO maternal health estimates

vulnerablePopulations: pregnant people with limited antenatal care access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Hypertension after 20 weeks with proteinuria and/or end-organ dysfunction; management per national/FOGSI protocols

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: ANC blood pressure screening is routine in public programmes, but quality and continuity vary
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; outcomes depend on early detection and timely referral to EmOC facilities

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: BP measurement and urine testing in antenatal visits; high-risk pregnancies referred to district/tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Low-dose aspirin for high-risk pregnancies, antihypertensives (labetalol/nifedipine), magnesium sulfate for severe pre-eclampsia/eclampsia prevention, and planned delivery
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Referral delays, limited ICU capacity in some districts, and uneven access to high-risk obstetric services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Widespread ANC platform and strong emphasis on magnesium sulfate availability in emergency obstetric care
